title: Fee updates can silently disable swaps
description: Updating fee parameters can result in a configuration that silently disables all swap operations. Certain fee values (e.g., 100% fee, or fee configurations that make swaps economically impossible) are not rejected during the update, leading to a state where the AMM appears active but no swaps can succeed.
recommendation: Validate fee parameter updates to ensure the resulting configuration still allows swap operations, rejecting updates that would effectively disable the AMM.

oracle.description: Fee update does not validate that the new fee configuration still permits functional swap operations.
oracle.static_signal: Fee update choice that sets fee parameters without bounds checking (e.g., fee < 100%, fee >= 0)

notes: Interesting pattern: a valid-looking administrative action that silently breaks core functionality.
